theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Energy (+2.63%) and Health Care (+1.27%) led the market today, while Technology (-2.08%) and Industrials (-1.82%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -0.73% versus +0.91% for defensives and +0.15% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 -0.45%, Dow -0.25%, Nasdaq -1.16% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.90%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX rose 3.60% to 16.13, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 0.71% to $4,112.30, crude oil was little changed at $72.49, and bitcoin slipped 0.22% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
